title: Jacobian Conjecture is false in dimension 3 (certified refutation of the formal-conjectures instance)

source_refs: P. Chojecki, A Counterexample to the Jacobian Conjecture (ulam.ai, 2026-07-20), Theorem 3.1 / Cor 3.2

informal_statement: The Jacobian Conjecture (Keller 1939) is FALSE in dimension 3: there is a polynomial map F : C^3 -> C^3 with a nonzero constant Jacobian determinant (a Keller map) that is not invertible. This packet certifies exactly the negated formal-conjectures instance `jacobian_conjecture` in dimension 3 - the explicit witness is F(x,y,z) = ((1+xy)^3 z + y^2(1+xy)(4+3xy), y + 3x(1+xy)^2 z + 3xy^2(4+3xy), 2x - 3x^2 y - x^3 z), with det Jac F = -2. It does NOT resolve the dimension-2 problem (open), the map's fiber/image geometry, general-n stabilization, or the Dixmier/Mathieu/Zhao/cubic-reduction bridges (open or only conditionally proved elsewhere).
informal_proof_idea: Instantiate the universally-quantified claim at the explicit Keller map F. Its Jacobian determinant is the nonzero constant -2 (kernel-checked: expand det via Matrix.det_fin_three, differentiate with pderiv, close with ring), so F satisfies the Keller hypothesis IsUnit (det Jac F). If F had a polynomial inverse G, evaluation composition would recover every point p from aeval p (F) via G; but F collapses the two distinct points (0,0,-1/4) and (1,-3/2,13/2) to the same image (-1/4,0,0) (kernel-checked by fin_cases + norm_num on the aeval evaluations), so recovering the first coordinate forces 0 = 1, a contradiction. Hence no such G exists and the instance is refuted.
formal_statement_pp:
theorem jacobian_conjecture_false_dim_three :
    ¬ (∀ F : Fin 3 → MvPolynomial (Fin 3) ℂ,
         IsUnit (Matrix.of fun i j => pderiv i (F j)).det →
         ∃ G : Fin 3 → MvPolynomial (Fin 3) ℂ,
           (fun i => bind₁ G (F i)) = X ∧ (fun i => bind₁ F (G i)) = X)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 1f9dfbee).
